exports.content: FA-dominated perovskite (FA0.93Cs0.07PbI3) with antisolvent-free deposition. Module structure: glass/FTO/SnO2/perovskite/passivator/spiro-OMeTAD/Au. Small devices (0.14 cm2), large devices (1.04 cm2), mini-modules (13.44 cm2), and fully slot-die printed large modules (310 cm2 and 802 cm2).
exports.label: exp_context

exports.content: FABr addition accelerates the formation of n=2 2D perovskite, appearing at 33 seconds during spin coating versus 40 seconds for DABr alone, enabling homogeneous coverage even at room temperature
exports.label: fabr_accelerated_growth

exports.content: Incorporating formamidinium bromide (FABr) into n-dodecylammonium halide (DAX) post-treatment enables growth of phase-pure n=2 2D perovskite capping layer with homogeneous morphology on 3D perovskite surfaces
exports.label: homogeneous_2d_passivation

exports.content: Champion active-area efficiency of 24.62% for 1.04 cm2 large devices, demonstrating less than 5% efficiency loss per ten times magnification
exports.label: large_cell_2462

exports.content: Champion active-area efficiency of 23.60% for 13.44 cm2 mini-modules, maintaining the small efficiency loss scaling trend
exports.label: mini_module_2360

exports.content: Encapsulated mini-modules exhibit remarkable operational stability with T80 lifetime exceeding 2000 hours at MPPT under continuous light illumination
exports.label: module_mppt_stability

exports.content: Phase separation occurs in double-halide alloyed 2D perovskites with long-chain (>10) alkylamine ligands, producing uneven n-value distribution on 3D perovskite surfaces. This energy disorder deteriorates interfacial charge transport in large-area modules.
exports.label: phase_problem

exports.content: Long-chain alkylamine ligands (>10 carbons) cause phase separation in double-halide alloyed 2D perovskites, while triple-halide compositions successfully eliminate the problematic phase separation
exports.label: phase_separation_discovery

exports.content: Fully slot-die printed large modules achieve champion aperture-area efficiency of 18.90% for 20 cm x 20 cm modules (310 cm2 aperture area)
exports.label: printed_module_310

exports.content: Fully slot-die printed modules achieve champion aperture-area efficiency of 17.59% for 30 cm x 30 cm modules (802 cm2 aperture area), demonstrating the feasibility of upscaling manufacturing
exports.label: printed_module_802

exports.content: Champion active-area efficiency of 25.61% for 0.14 cm2 small devices using the DABr/FABr homogeneous 2D passivation strategy with antisolvent-free processing
exports.label: small_cell_2561

exports.content: Triple-halide compositions (I/Br/Cl) have lower formation enthalpy than double-halide alloys for n=2 2D perovskite, enabling preferential formation of phase-pure n=2 structure confirmed by in situ PL and GIWAXS
exports.label: triple_halide_mechanism

exports.content: The FABr-assisted homogeneous passivation strategy is universal, successfully suppressing phase separation for most conventional 2D ligands including BA, OA, DA, PMA, PEA, and NMA
exports.label: universal_ligand
